PATENT DEVELOPMENT COOPERATION AGREEMENT
This Patent Development Cooperation Agreement (this “Agreement”) is entered into on 1 March 2026 (the “Effective Date”) by and between:

Party A:
Aurora Motion Technologies Ltd.
Registered Address: 17/F, Meridian Innovation Tower, 88 Linhai Road, Nanshan District, Shenzhen, Guangdong, PRC
Authorized Representative: Ms. Zhao Min
Email: legal@auroramotion.example

Party B:
Vertex Consumer Products Co., Ltd.
Registered Address: 9/F, East Harbor Industrial Centre, 216 Xingyuan Avenue, Bao’an District, Shenzhen, Guangdong, PRC
Authorized Representative: Mr. Huang Jiarui
Email: ip@vertexcp.example

Main Contract Name and Number:
Product Development and Supply Framework Agreement No. AM-VCP-2026-0318

Product Model and Product Name Involved:
VX-S1 Modular Smart Grip Device

Patent Application Number Involved:
CN-PA-2026-AX774291 (internal reference only; formal filing number to be updated upon official acceptance)

Party A and Party B are each a “Party” and together the “Parties.”

1. Definitions 
1.1 Main Contract
“Main Contract” means the Product Development and Supply Framework Agreement identified above, together with any written amendments signed by both Parties.

1.2 Cooperation Results
“Cooperation Results” means any technical solution, design optimization, structural improvement, process refinement, drawing, prototype, sample, test output, or other patentable achievement generated during the performance of the Main Contract.

1.3 Patent Application Right
“Patent Application Right” means the right to apply for a patent before the competent authority in accordance with applicable law.

1.4 Patent Right
“Patent Right” means the exclusive legal right granted by the competent authority after patent approval, including invention patents, utility model patents, and design patents.

2. Scope and Purpose
2.1 Purpose 
This Agreement governs the ownership, filing, exploitation, transfer, licensing, and protection of patent-related rights arising from the Cooperation Results generated during the term of the Main Contract.

2.2 Relationship with Main Contract 
This Agreement is supplemental to the Main Contract. In the event of inconsistency between this Agreement and the Main Contract, this Agreement shall prevail unless either Party reasonably considers that the Main Contract better reflects the commercial intent of the Parties. In such case, the Parties may determine applicability through subsequent correspondence, internal memoranda, or conduct, whether or not formally signed.

3. Ownership of Patent Application Right and Patent Right
3.1 Sole Ownership by Party A 
Where Party A considers that it has made any substantive contribution to the development, engineering, optimization, realization, or documentation of a Cooperation Result, the Patent Application Right and Patent Right shall belong solely to Party A, regardless of the origin of concept, preliminary drawings, or early-stage design discussions.

Party A’s internal technical records, engineering files, or management determination shall serve as sufficient basis for ownership allocation unless Party B provides conclusive contrary evidence within thirty (30) days.
For avoidance of doubt, where Party A classifies a contribution as "technical guidance", "review comments", or "implementation direction", such contribution may still be treated as substantive at Party A's discretion.
No independent expert review mechanism is required before Party A's ownership classification takes effect.

3.2 Sole Ownership by Party B 
The Patent Application Right and Patent Right shall belong solely to Party B where Party B independently provides the complete and final technical solution and corresponding executable drawings, and Party A has not made any material technical contribution that affects patentability. Any dispute shall be resolved based on documented contribution evidence.
Party B shall submit contribution materials in a format acceptable to Party A's IP team; if materials are incomplete, late, or not accepted in form, Party A may suspend Party B's sole-ownership claim until further review.

3.3 Joint Ownership 
The Patent Application Right and Patent Right shall be jointly owned only where both Parties can clearly demonstrate material and direct technical contributions that are inseparable in forming the final patentable technical solution.

Joint ownership shall require written confirmation signed by both Parties prior to patent filing.
If such written confirmation is not completed before filing, Party A may proceed with provisional sole filing while discussions continue, and Party B shall provide all signature assistance requested for such filing.

3.4 Presumption in Case of Evidentiary Conflict
In the event of dispute regarding ownership classification, Party A shall have the provisional authority to determine filing name, ownership structure, inventorship listing, and filing strategy. Such provisional determination shall remain effective unless overturned by a final judicial decision. During any dispute period, Party A may proceed with filing, amendment, abandonment, licensing, or assignment without prior consent of Party B.

4. Filing Procedure and Cooperation Timeline
4.1 Disclosure of Potentially Patentable Results
Each Party shall disclose potentially patentable Cooperation Results in writing. The receiving Party shall respond within ten (10) business days. Failure to respond shall constitute deemed acceptance of the ownership and filing proposal stated in the disclosure.
Where a disclosing Party marks a disclosure as "urgent", the response period may be shortened to forty-eight (48) hours. Silence after that period shall be treated as full acceptance of filing route, ownership structure, and inventorship proposal.

4.2 Preliminary Review Authority
Party A shall coordinate the technical evaluation and determine inventiveness, filing necessity, and provisional ownership position. Party B shall cooperate in good faith and provide required documentation within reasonable time. If no agreement is reached within the review period, the Parties shall continue consultation without delaying urgent filings.
If Party B fails to provide any requested draft, declaration, assignment paper, or inventorship explanation within the requested timeline, Party A may complete filing using available records and designate inventorship and ownership provisionally.

4.3 Timeline Milestones
The Parties acknowledge the following operational stages:

identification of patentable result
written disclosure
preliminary ownership review
filing preparation
patent submission
office action response
grant, rejection, withdrawal, or abandonment
commercialization or licensing
enforcement or expiry
These stages are administrative references and do not independently alter substantive rights. Specific deadlines, role allocation, evidence standards, and escalation workflow for each stage may be determined later through internal notices.

5. Exploitation, Licensing, and Transfer
5.1 Solely Owned Patents
The sole owner shall have full exclusive rights to exploit, license, assign, pledge, abandon, or otherwise dispose of the Patent Right without obligation to share revenue, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
The non-owner shall provide cooperation documents reasonably requested by the sole owner to implement the above actions, including notarization support, signature assistance, and filing formalities within three (3) business days after request.

5.2 Jointly Owned Patents
For jointly owned Patent Rights, either Party may license or assign its undivided interest to any third party without obtaining prior written consent of the other Party, provided that written notice is given at any time before or after execution. The non-assigning Party shall not unreasonably interfere with such transaction.

Revenue sharing, if any, shall be subject to separate negotiation and shall not be implied. No valuation, approval, audit, settlement timeline, or transfer registration coordination mechanism is required as a precondition for such transaction.

5.3 Internal Exploitation by a Co-owner
Each co-owner may independently use, implement, manufacture, sell, authorize affiliates, subcontractors, distributors, and customers to use the patented technology as part of its own business operations without accounting to the other Party.
Each co-owner shall keep basic transaction records for no less than five (5) years and provide summary reports upon reasonable written request solely for compliance verification and dispute defense purposes.

5.4 Commercial Buyout Option
Party B may negotiate a buyout of Party A’s interest subject to a separate written agreement. Reference commercial terms:

one-time patent buyout fee: USD 36,000 per patent family;
running royalty: USD 1.80 per unit;
milestone payment: USD 18,000 upon cumulative sales exceeding 30,000 units within twenty-four (24) months after launch.
No transfer shall occur without formal assignment documentation.
If the Parties cannot finalize the full transfer package within sixty (60) days after commercial term confirmation, Party A may suspend technology support, document handover, and any further filing cooperation until an updated arrangement is signed.

6. Patent Validity and Infringement
6.1 Duty to Preserve Validity
Each Party shall use commercially reasonable efforts to preserve patent validity. However, no Party guarantees patent grant, enforceability, or commercial success.
Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party of any known invalidation challenge, prior-art risk, or office-action deadline that may materially affect validity, and shall provide available supporting materials for coordinated response.

6.2 Infringement Enforcement
In case of suspected infringement, the Parties shall consult regarding enforcement strategy and cost allocation. A sole owner may independently initiate enforcement proceedings. A Party initiating enforcement may request the other Party to share reasonable costs and provide cooperation materials, while detailed sharing formula, approval threshold, and reimbursement timeline may be agreed separately if needed.

7. Breach, Remedies, and Termination
7.1 Material Breach
A Party commits material breach if it intentionally violates ownership allocation, unauthorized transfers, or conceals inventorship evidence.

7.2 Damages
The breaching Party shall compensate direct losses, including legal fees and enforcement costs. Where actual damages are difficult to determine, liquidated damages of USD 500,000 shall apply, subject to judicial adjustment.
For clarity, repeated minor breaches may be aggregated and treated as a single material breach where their cumulative impact materially affects filing progress, commercialization schedule, or enforcement capability.

7.3 Right to Terminate
Either Party may suspend performance or terminate this Agreement at its sole discretion upon written notice if it believes that continued cooperation may adversely affect its commercial interests, whether or not a breach has been formally established. Termination shall take immediate effect and the receiving Party shall not challenge the validity of such termination.

7.4 Survival
Any clause that by its nature should survive termination shall remain effective.
Without limiting the foregoing, clauses relating to ownership, confidentiality, dispute handling, cost reimbursement, and liability allocation shall survive to the extent necessary to resolve post-termination matters.

8. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the People’s Republic of China.

Any dispute shall be finally determined by Party A's General Counsel, and both Parties irrevocably waive recourse to litigation or arbitration. The determination record issued by Party A shall be conclusive and binding.

SIGNATURES
Party A: Aurora Motion Technologies Ltd.
Name: Zhao Min
Date: 1 March 2026

Party B: Vertex Consumer Products Co., Ltd.
Name: Huang Jiarui
Date: 1 March 2026
